af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 85) with 102 tools for codebase analysis. Local structural analysis engine — no external dependencies, air-gapped capable. No authentication needed. Initial index build scales with codebase size (minutes for large repos). Index must be rebuilt/updated when files change — stale index produces incorrect blast radius analysis. 102 MCP tools creates tool-selection overhead for agents. fastmcp dependency required for MCP mode but not default pip install.

security_score_components.security_notes: Fully local execution — no external network calls, no telemetry. Security relies on local OS access controls. SQLite-based data store. No credentials needed. Air-gapped operation possible — ideal for sensitive codebases. Python dependencies should be audited. No API surface to secure externally.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — no SLA. Index freshness is critical: stale index gives wrong dependency/blast radius data. Incremental update support available. 102 tools cover 27 languages but Tier 2 language quality (generic tree-sitter) varies. Retry guidance not documented. No pagination needed (graph queries return structured results).

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Self-hosted open-source team messaging platform. auth_complexity_score=60 — two-part auth (X-Auth-Token + X-User-Id headers) required on every request, unusual pattern. Bot users should use Personal Access Tokens to avoid creating sessions from username/password login. WebSocket realtime API uses DDP protocol, separate from REST. Rate limiting is server-side configuration — varies between deployments. Webhook integrations use a script-based system.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted — TLS is operator responsibility. Personal Access Tokens for bots (better than session auth). GDPR, HIPAA, ISO27001 compliant. User roles determine access but API keys don't have per-endpoint scoping. PAT via environment variables. Active open source project with security focus.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — reliability depends on operator infrastructure. Cloud hosted option with SLA available. Stable REST API. WebSocket DDP realtime API is separate and requires different client handling. Retry guidance not documented. Rate limits vary by server configuration. Offset-based pagination.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 62). Bridges AI models with ROS/ROS2 robots. No authentication — relies on local network and ROS environment access controls. auth_complexity_score=100 (no auth needed, but security relies entirely on network isolation). Requires rosbridge node running in ROS environment. IMPORTANT: Publishing to actuator topics can cause physical robot movement — agents must exercise extreme caution. ROS1 vs ROS2 configuration differences poorly documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: IMPORTANT SAFETY CONCERN: No authentication layer. Any process connected to the local network can send commands that cause physical robot movement. Must be used only in isolated, trusted environments. Relies entirely on network segmentation for security. No TLS documented. ROS environment access controls are the only security boundary. Not suitable for any networked or multi-tenant deployment.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on rosbridge node availability in ROS environment. ROS1/ROS2 configuration differences undocumented. Early project with limited production track record. Physical robot safety depends on agent correctness — no guardrails. No retry guidance. No SLA. Appropriate for research and controlled robotics environments.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 72). Multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code built on Rust/WebAssembly. Requires Claude Code installed first — not standalone. Relies on underlying LLM provider API keys (Anthropic, etc.). Complex swarm topologies risk runaway agent spawning without careful configuration. Performance claims (352x speedup, 89% routing accuracy) are self-reported. PostgreSQL vector DB dependency adds infrastructure overhead. Node.js 20+ required. Documentation quality is limited.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for LLM provider API calls. Bcrypt-hardened credential handling for local credentials. LLM API keys via environment variables. No native auth layer for the MCP server itself. Rust/WASM codebase — verify all dependencies. PostgreSQL for vector storage needs separate security hardening. No formal security audit.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local Claude Code dependency means reliability tied to Claude Code's operation. Complex swarm topologies can create runaway agent cascades. Byzantine fault-tolerant consensus is claimed but unverified. Early project with limited production track record. WASM platform-specific build issues possible.

af_score_components.notes: Peripheral MCP support (quality 20). Lightweight embedded Go rule engine framework — not an MCP server by design. Agents interact via embedded Go SDK or REST, not MCP protocol. Zero external middleware dependencies. No built-in auth — security is the embedding application's responsibility. JavaScript transformation components require embedded JS engine. Hot-reload of rule chains can cause in-flight message loss if not handled carefully. Go-only SDK.

security_score_components.security_notes: Embedded library — TLS and auth are the responsibility of the embedding application. No built-in auth means deploying as a service requires additional security layers. Zero external middleware dependencies reduces attack surface. Go language with open source codebase. Upstream data source credentials managed by host application.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Embedded library — reliability depends on host application. Go with stable semver versioning. Hot-reload during active rule processing requires careful state management to avoid message loss. No external dependencies means fewer failure points. No SLA as open source embedded library.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. AI video generation platform (Gen-3 Alpha and others). Bearer token auth — paid plan required, no free API tier. Generation takes 1-3 minutes — agents must implement async polling. Content policy enforcement can reject prompts without detailed explanation. Generated videos expire — must download promptly. API features lag behind Runway web UI. Task queue latency highly variable during peak demand. Image-to-video requires specific preprocessing.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Bearer token authentication with paid plan requirement. No fine-grained scope control. Commercial AI platform — data handling per privacy policy. API key via environment variables. Generated video output URLs expire — ephemeral storage design.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. Gen-3 model is current but model generations evolve. API is separate from web UI — features may lag. Task queue variable latency during high demand. No retry guidance documented. Generation is async (1-3 min) — requires robust polling. Non-idempotent generation outputs.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Enterprise CRM platform. auth_complexity_score=35 — OAuth2 with multiple flows; JWT bearer best for agents but requires Salesforce admin to create Connected App; complex setup. SOQL has strict limitations (no full-text search, no aggregation in WHERE). API call limits (100k/day) can be exhausted by aggressive agents. API version must be specified in URL. Governor limits in Apex restrict endpoint capabilities. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. OAuth2/JWT bearer with extremely granular Salesforce authorization objects, profiles, and permission sets. SOC1, SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP, HIPAA, GDPR compliant — highest compliance tier. JWT private keys, OAuth tokens via secure env vars. Connected App OAuth scopes provide additional control. Enterprise-grade security architecture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: trust.salesforce.com with real-time status, FedRAMP/SOC2 SLA. Versioned URLs per API version — specify version explicitly. Retry guidance documented. API limits require careful management in automation. Governor limits add complexity. Offset pagination. Enterprise-grade reliability — core business infrastructure for millions of companies.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 76). Flexible headless CMS with GROQ query language. Robot tokens available for agent use. GROQ is Sanity's custom query language — agents must learn it rather than SQL/GraphQL. Mutations use transaction format (array of operations), not single-operation REST. Draft documents have 'drafts.' ID prefix — must exclude from published content queries. Image/file uploads are two-step process (asset then document mutation). Real-time listeners via WebSocket.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Project-level tokens with read/write/manage roles. Robot tokens for agents. OAuth available for user-context operations. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. Token permissions coarse-grained at project level — no per-document access control. Robot token via environment variables. Webhook signature validation available.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. Stable API with GROQ language stability. Real-time WebSocket listeners for live updates. Cursor-based pagination. Retry guidance not documented. Draft/published document distinction requires careful agent logic. Mutations as transaction arrays provide atomicity. Generally reliable managed platform.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Enterprise ERP OData APIs. auth_complexity_score=25 (most complex auth of any commonly evaluated API) — OAuth2 via XSUAA service + Basic Auth for legacy + SAML + CSRF tokens required for writes + VPN for on-premise + Basis team configuration required. OData v2/v4 query syntax ($filter, $expand) required. SAP landscape has DEV/QAS/PRD systems. Authorization objects extremely granular — missing auth causes opaque 403 errors. API Hub sandbox behavior differs from production.

security_score_components.security_notes: SAP BTP enforces HTTPS; on-premise connectivity requires VPN/RFC. OAuth2 via XSUAA service with fine-grained SAP authorization objects. SOC1, SOC2, ISO27001, HIPAA, GDPR compliant. Extremely granular permission model. Enterprise key management via XSUAA. No hardcoded credentials in application code by SAP design. Best-in-class enterprise security.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise-grade SLA, SOC2. OData v2/v4 differences require version awareness. CSRF token requirement adds retry complexity. Landscape-specific configuration means DEV/QAS/PRD behavior varies. On-premise connectivity adds failure point. Retry guidance not documented. Offset pagination. Generally reliable for organizations with proper SAP Basis support.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Enterprise cloud test automation platform. HTTP Basic Auth with username + access key. US and EU data centers have different API base URLs and credentials — agents must target correct region. Multiple product lines (VDC, RDC, API Testing) with different endpoint namespaces. Job assets (screenshots, videos, logs) deleted after 30 days. SauceConnect tunnel daemon required for localhost testing. Test result status must be explicitly set via API — auto-detection unreliable.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. HTTP Basic Auth with username + access key. Team access management available. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. Separate US/EU credentials provide some regional isolation. Access key via environment variables. No fine-grained per-operation scoping.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise SLA, SOC2. Stable mature API. Job assets expire after 30 days — download required. Test status must be explicitly set. SauceConnect tunnel adds operational dependency. Retry guidance not documented. US/EU region routing must be correct. Offset pagination.
